title: CSS Best Practices Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential rules for writing clean, maintainable, and high-performance CSS.

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a professional-grade web application?
options: Table Layouts
Float Layouts
Using a consistent naming convention like BEM (Block Element Modifier)
Absolute Positioning only

questions.question: Which of these is a major 'Pro' of using CSS shorthand properties compared to writing them separately?
options: It makes your CSS files smaller and more readable.
It allows users to print only the core content.
It is always supported by every browser.
It replaces the need for any HTML5 tags.

questions.question: What is the primary role of 'Minification' in a CSS production build?
options: To make the site load much faster by removing all extra spaces and comments to reduce file size.
To choose the colors for the animation.
To automatically generate CSS animations.
To hide code from search engines.

questions.question: Why is it important to almost ALWAYS avoid 'Inlined Styles' (style='...') on elements in a professional website?
options: They are difficult to maintain and overwrite due to very high specificity.
They make the HTML file too small.
They are not supported by modern browsers.
They take up no space in the layout.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a best practice for a professional-style stylesheet?
options: Commenting your code.
Using a reset or normalize stylesheet.
Using 'important' on every single rule to ensure it always wins.
Using variables for colors and spacing.
